destinations.evidence: Both node executor construction paths call the worker availability check and convert failures into startup errors.
The worker check resolves RITUAL_JQ_WORKER_BIN or the adjacent binary, verifies existence, warms the pool, and errors when missing.

equivalence_argument: JQ worker startup validation lives in the nodebuilder workspace for this port line; both exposed build_evm paths abort before node startup proceeds if the binary cannot be resolved.
notes: The port checkout itself has no jq worker code; nodebuilder is the correct owner for this runtime precompile wiring.
rationale: The primary diff adds ensure_jq_worker_available(), calls it from both executor builder paths, and maps missing-binary errors to node startup abortion. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Both RitualExecutorBuilder build_evm paths must call ensure_jq_worker_available() and abort node startup with an error if the ritual-jq-worker binary cannot be resolved and found.
suggested_falsification_tests: Start the node with RITUAL_JQ_WORKER_BIN pointing to a missing path and assert build_evm returns a startup error before block execution; start with a valid worker path and assert build_evm proceeds.
summary: JQ worker availability is enforced at node startup.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Set RITUAL_JQ_WORKER_BIN to a missing path and assert nodebuilder build_evm returns an error before block execution.
title: JQ worker required at startup
